factors.name: Dow Record Close
factors.summary: Dow closed at 53,061.30 (+0.29%), hitting an all-time record. Boeing (+3.55%), IBM (+3.45%), and Goldman Sachs (+3.36%) led, showing breadth in heavy-weight industrials and financials that power the price-weighted index.

factors.name: Chip Sector Strength
factors.summary: Samsung profit surged 19-fold on AI memory chip demand; Micron gained; SK Hynix ADR listing imminent. Broadcom–Apple partnership extended to 2031. Syntiant filing IPO. This benefits Dow tech/industrial exposure and confidence in capex cycles.

factors.name: Fed Policy Rhetoric
factors.summary: Fed's Waller reaffirmed 2% inflation commitment; Warsh stripped forward guidance from statement. Internal split between Waller and Warsh on guidance stance. Wednesday Fed minutes release will clarify direction. No imminent rate surprise evident; markets pricing elevated rates for longer (per FX commentary).

factors.name: US Services & Labor Data
factors.summary: US services data described as 'resilient' by FX analysts. Prior soft jobs report last Friday initially weighed on stocks, but equities recovered Monday into close. Suggests labor market cooling without collapse — favorable for equities.

factors.name: Japan Household Spending & Wages
factors.summary: May 2026 Japan household spending +3.7% m/m (beat +1.4% expected), but wages +3.2% y/y missed +3.4% expected and came below prior +3.5%. Strong consumption offset wage miss, supporting global growth narrative but adding wage-pressure nuance.

factors.name: Oil & Energy
factors.summary: Crude settled down $0.14 at $68.55; Saudi Arabia cut prices, signaling oversupply. Strait of Hormuz tanker attack reported but minimal impact. Lower energy costs benefit Dow's transportation/industrial users (negative for CVX but positive for cyclical costs). Pre-war oil levels suggest energy shock receding.

factors.name: Manufacturing & Industrial
factors.summary: No fresh ISM Manufacturing or PMI data in this window. Germany DAX/Italy FTSE MIB closed at records; other EU indices lower. Mixed global manufacturing backdrop. Awaiting Tuesday Japan wage data and any US factory data for clearer industrial signal.

factors.name: Dollar & FX
factors.summary: USD/JPY grinding higher (JPY hedge funds most negative since 2007); GBP/JPY at 18-year highs (217+). DXY near 100.90. Traders most positive on dollar since 2015 per Bloomberg. Strong dollar pressures multinational Dow earnings translation but supports capital flows into US assets.

factors.name: Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: Rivian forecast Q revenue above estimates (despite 75M share dilution offering). Microsoft cutting 4,800 jobs (Xbox restructure, cost control signal). Lockheed Martin and L3Harris won major defense contracts. Mixed but earnings surprises largely beat or in-line; cost discipline evident.

factors.name: Consumer & Retail
factors.summary: Walmart cutting summer prices across stores; Trump cited beef price cuts. Price deflation in consumer staples is Fed-friendly and consumer-positive. Trump Accounts launched July 4; families engaging in new investment vehicle. Retail confidence steady, not distressed.

factors.name: Geopolitical / Supply Chain
factors.summary: Iran conflict receding per ECB's Schnabel (oil already repriced); Canada pivoting to faster Clearwater oil drilling; Ukraine refinery strikes ongoing but not market-moving. Strait of Hormuz tanker incident minor. Supply chains normalizing post-Iran shock.

factors.name: Valuation & Sentiment
factors.summary: MarketWatch cautioned that equity valuations still extreme vs. history, though recent earnings growth has diverged upward from trend. Hedge funds most positive on dollar since 2015; KKR private credit redemptions slowing (risk appetite intact). Sentiment stretched but not panicked.

screening.narrative: Dow hit an all-time record close (53,061.30) on Monday, driven by chip sector strength (Samsung, Broadcom, Micron), energy cost relief, and solid earnings surprises in industrials and defense. Fed rhetoric remains hawkish but not shocking; services data resilient. The move is +0.29% in a single session and +0.46% in 24h—modest but in a new-record phase, suggesting Phase 2 momentum underway. Oil at pre-war lows removes energy headwind; dollar strength supports capital flows into US. However, Wednesday's Fed minutes loom; upcoming ISM and earnings will test conviction.
catalysts_ahead: Wed 7 Jul: Federal Reserve minutes from Warsh's first meeting (forward guidance intent)
Wed 7 Jul: Japan wage data (Tuesday evening Asia time) — monitoring sustainability of labor cost pressures
Thu 8 Jul: US ISM Manufacturing PMI (critical for Dow industrial composition sentiment)
Fri 9 Jul: US NFP / unemployment report (labor market health confirmation, rate-path implications)
Next week: Major Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, CAT, BA, HD)
China PMI and growth data (multinational capex confidence signals)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed minutes signal surprise hawkishness or rate-hike extension, or if ISM/NFP misses sharply, the record-high momentum could reverse sharply. Alternatively, if chip sector earnings disappoint (SK Hynix ADR, Samsung next quarter guidance), or if oil rebounds above $75 (geopolitical shock), Dow's industrials could face margin pressure. Valuation is stretched; any earnings miss or growth slowdown signal could trigger a 2–3% pullback. Tariff/trade policy surprises under Trump administration also remain a tail risk for multinational revenue.
